agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences for users. Bird produced two iterations of requirements — v2 supersedes v1 after a legal review removed the transactional email opt-out capability. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's v2. The scope is narrow: allow users to control marketing emails only; transactional emails are non-negotiable and must be enforced at the API level.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled=false must be rejected — the system must never persist this state"},{"rule":"Marketing emails are user-controllable","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false, and the persisted value reflects their choice"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences endpoint","implementation_note":"Use PATCH semantics — the client sends only the fields it wants to change. Validate the full resulting state before persisting."},{"decision":"Marketing opt-out stored as boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default value should be true (opted-in). Confirm with Bird if a different default is intended — not explicitly stated."},{"decision":"Reject transactional_emails_enabled=false with 403","implementation_note":"Return HTTP 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. This is Bird's explicit AC3 from v2. The field may still appear in the API schema for read purposes, but writes of false are forbidden."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled=true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back into marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled=false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email disable attempt is rejected","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED"},{"criterion":"Transactional email enable is a no-op success","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK (value was already true, no state change)"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated emails such as password resets and security alerts. Legally required — cannot be disabled by the user."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. Fully user-controllable."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 AC2 allowed users to opt out of transactional emails, but Bird v1's own domain rule said transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. Internal contradiction in v1.","resolution":"Bird v2 resolves this definitively: AC2 was removed after legal review. Transactional email opt-out is not permitted, period. v2 supersedes v1. MJ's architecture already aligns with v2."}],"open_questions":["What is the default value of marketing_emails_enabled for new users? Assumed true (opted-in) but not explicitly stated by Bird.","Should the PATCH endpoint accept transactional_emails_enabled=true silently, or should the field be excluded from the writable schema entirely?","Authorization model: can a user only update their own preferences, or can admins update on behalf of users?"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are unambiguous after Bird v2 — transactional emails are non-negotiable","MJ's architecture aligns cleanly with Bird's v2 requirements","AC3 (403 rejection) is well-specified by both Bird and MJ"],"low_confidence_areas":["Default value for marketing_emails_enabled not explicitly stated","Whether transactional_emails_enabled should appear in the writable schema at all or only as a read-only field"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 fully supersedes v1 — no elements of v1 survive unless restated in v2","marketing_emails_enabled defaults to true for new users","The 403 + error code pattern is the correct rejection mechanism (both Bird and MJ agree, but no broader API standards doc was provided)"]}}
